text:
If you were hoping today would clear out the smoke haze, hold that thought for Sunday. Air quality is flagged unhealthy for sensitive groups again this afternoon as wildfire smoke drifts back in, layered right on top of a soggy, unsettled day. Showers are likely from late morning on, with thunderstorms firing up between about 2 and 5pm and lingering into the evening, high near 81. The models are arguing about just how wet: the American model keeps it fairly modest, but the European and Canadian guidance both go all in on a washout, so I'd plan around getting soaked, not just sprinkled on. The lake's under a wind advisory too, south winds building into the 15 to 25 knot range with waves 1 to 3 feet, so pleasure boaters should be off the broad waters well before those afternoon storms arrive. Tonight's rain continues into the early morning hours before clearing out behind a west wind shift.

Sunday flips the script entirely, mostly sunny, high near 77, lighter winds, and better air. If you've got outdoor plans, save them for tomorrow.
